{{DISPLAYTITLE:iNSANE Summer Split}} {{Infobox league |game=bgmi |liquipediatier=4 |name=iNSANE Summer Split |icon= |tickername=ISS |shortname=ISS |image=INSANE Summer Split.png |type=Online |team_number=19 |series=iNSANE Summer Split |organizer= TEAM iNSANE |organizer-link=https://www.instagram.com/teaminsane_esports/ |sponsor=[https://www.instagram.com/teaminsane_esports/ TEAM iNSANE] |mode=squads |prizepoolusd= |perspective=tpp |localcurrency=inr |prizepool=1,00,000 |country=India |sdate=2021-09-23 |edate=2021-09-25 |instagram=teaminsane_esports |web= https://teaminsane.gg/ |youtube=channel/UCJxRJMlXCWRIOk4L0CYpKuQ |twitter= |discord=4b8ThjfwdM }} '''[https://www.instagram.com/teaminsane_esports/ TEAM iNSANE]''' present a BGMI invitational tournament called the '''''iNSANE Summer Split''''' with a prize pool of ₹1,00,000 INR. ==About== ===Broadcast Talent=== {{box|start|padding=1em}} {{broadcasterCard |position=Hindi Commentator |lang=in |b1=HornetFear|b1flag=in|b1name=Prashant Shukla |b2=Raider|b2flag=in|b2name=Shashank Singh }} {{box|end}} ===Format=== '''''iNSANE Summer Split:''' 25th August 2021 to 26th September 2021.'' '''''Pre-Qualifiers:''' 25th August 2021 to 4th September 2021'' * '' A total of 2000 teams will play in Pre-Qualifiers * '' Teams to be divided into 100 groups''. * '' Each team will get to play a single match'' * '' Top 8 Teams from each group will qualify for the Qualifiers''. * {{bgcolortext|up|''Top 800 teams}} advance to Qualifiers.''
'''''Qualifiers:''' 6th September 2021 to 9th September 2021'' * '' A total of 800 teams will play in Qualifiers * '' Teams to be divided into 40 groups''. * '' Each team will get to play a single match'' * '' Top 6 Teams from each group will qualify for the Group Stages''. * {{bgcolortext|up|''Top 240 teams}} advance to the Group Stages.''
'''''Group Stages:''' 11th September 2021 to 12th September 2021'' * '' A total of 240 teams will play in Group Stages * '' Teams to be divided into 12 groups''. * '' Each team will get to play a total of 2 matches'' * '' Top 4 Teams from each group will qualify for the Quarter-Finals''. * {{bgcolortext|up|''Top 48 teams}} advance to the Quarter-Finals.''
'''''Quarter-Finals:''' 14th September 2021 to 15th September 2021'' * '' A total of 60 teams (48 Qualified + 12 Invited) will play in Quarter-Finals * '' Teams to be divided into 3 groups''. * '' Each team will get to play a total of 3 matches'' * '' Top 9 Teams from each group will qualify for the Semi Finals''. * {{bgcolortext|up|''Top 27 teams}} advance to Group Stages.''
'''''Semi Finals:''' 16th September 2021 to 17th September 2021'' * '' A total of 40 teams (27 Qualified + 13 Invited) will play in the Semi Finals'' * '' Teams to be divided into 2 groups''. * '' Each team will get to play a total of 4 matches'' * '' Top 3 Teams from each group will qualify for the Finals''. * {{bgcolortext|up|''Top 6 teams}} will advance to the Finals.''
'''''Finals:''' 19th September 2021 to 21st September 2021'' * '' A total of 20 teams (6 Qualified + 14 Invited) will play in the Finals'' * '' Each team will get to play a total of 9 matches'' * {{bgcolortext|up|''Top 3 teams}} will advance to the Grand Finals.''
'''''Grand Finals:''' 23rd September 2021 to 25th September 2021'' * '' A total of 18 teams (3 Qualified + 16 Invited) will play in the Grand Finals'' * '' Teams will get to play a total of 16 matches in a period of 4 days'' * '' Each day will feature a total of 4 matches'' ====Matchdays==== * ''3 Matchdays: 4 Matches Each Day.'' ** '''''Map Rotation''''': ''E>>M>>S>>M ====Points Distribution==== {{{title|For details on the points matrix, '''Click here'''.}}}

The League Stage of the tournament features a prize-pool of ₹15,00,000 {{Abbr/INR}} ==About== ===Broadcast Talent=== {{box|start|padding=1em}} {{broadcasterCard |position= Hindi Commentator |lang=in |b1=HornetFear |b2=Smart Wolf|b2flag=in|b2name=Harsh Gohil |b3=SardarjiYT |b4=HomeShow }} {{box|end}} ===Format=== '''''LOCO War of Glory League Stage:''' 5th October 2021 to 22nd October 2021.'' * ''Maps: {{Abbr/TPP}} [[Erangel]], [[Miramar]] and [[Sanhok]] '' * ''27 Teams divided into 3 groups '' (A, B, and C) * '' 9 Teams in each group'' * ''Teams will compete in a round robin format'' * ''99 Matches to be played over a period of 3 weeks (Each team to play 66 Matches).'' * ''27 Matches to be played over a period of 5 days for Week-1 (Each team to play 18 Matches).'' * ''36 Matches to be played over a period of 6 days for Week 2 & 3 (Each team to play 24 Matches).'' * ''{{bgcolortext|up|'''Top 18 teams '''}} from the overall standings will advance to [[LOCO_War_of_Glory/Grand_Finals|Grand Finals]]''. ====Matchdays==== * '''''Week 1''''' ** ''5 Matchdays: 6 Matches on Day 1 & 2 and 5 Matches for the rest of the days.'' * '''''Week 2 & 3''''' ** ''6 Matchdays: 6 Matches to be played each day.'' ** ''2x (AB + CA + BC)'' ====Points Distribution==== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" ! colspan="2"|Points Distribution |- |'''''Finish Points''''' ||''1 per finish'' |- !width=100px|Place !width=100px|Points |- |{{Placement|1}}||15 |- |{{Placement|2}}||12 |- |{{Placement|3}}||10 |- |{{Placement|4}}||8 |- |{{Placement|5}}||6 |- |{{Placement|6}}||4 |- |{{Placement|7}}||2 |- |{{Placement|8-15}}||1 |- |{{Placement|16-18}}||0 |} ===Prize Pool=== {{box|start|padding=2em|}} ₹ 15,00,000 INR (≃$20,229 USD) are spread among the teams as seen below:

{{GameSeries_Navbox}} {{Infobox company |name=KRAFTON |image=KRAFTON_lightmode.png |imagedark=KRAFTON_darkmode.png |location=South Korea |parent= |industry=Games Development |headquarters={{Flag|kr}} Seongnam |employees=2,100+ |site=https://www.krafton.com/en/ |twitter= |facebook=Krafton.official |instagram=krafton.today |youtube=KRAFTONofficial |twitch= }}
'''KRAFTON''' is a South Korean company that develops and distributes video games, particularly the main installments of [https://global.battlegrounds.pubg.com/ PUBG: Battlegrounds] for PC platform and [https://newstate.pubg.com/en PUBG: New State] for Mobile platform.
Formerly known as '''Bluehole''' and '''Krafton Game Union''', it also has many subsidary companies such as ''Bluehole Studios'', ''PUBG Studios'', and ''Rising Wings'' in which each subsidary is responsible for the further development of various games. ==Notable Events== {{TournamentsList|organizer={{PAGENAME}}|tier1=1}} ==References== {{Reflist}}

{{DISPLAYTITLE:NEW STATE MOBILE}} {{GameSeries_Navbox}} {{Infobox game |image=NEW_STATE_MOBILE_lightmode.png |imagedark=NEW_STATE_MOBILE_darkmode.png |name=NEW STATE MOBILE |developer=[[KRAFTON]] |releasedate1=2021-11-11 |platform1=Google Android |platform2=Apple iOS |website=https://newstate.pubg.com/en |instagram=newstatemobileofficial |twitter=NEWSTATEMOBILE |facebook=OfficialNEWSTATEMOBILE |discord=newstate |youtube2=UCnEaSfPj9ETzrySiRSD1Q1g }}
'''''NEW STATE MOBILE''''' (originally known as '''''PUBG: NEW STATE''''') is an online multiplayer mobile battle royale game developed and published by [[KRAFTON]]. It was released on November 11th, 2021 globally on iOS and Android platforms. It is the second installment in the [https://pubguniverse.com/ PUBG Universe], separated from [[pubg:PLAYERUNKNOWN%27S_BATTLEGROUNDS|PUBG: Battlegrounds]] and its [[PUBG Mobile|mobile]] version. ==Trivia== ''NEW STATE MOBILE'' is set as the sequel to [[PUBG Mobile]] in terms of its lore as it takes place 30 years into the future. ==Events== {{TournamentsList|game=ns|showTier=true}} ==External Links== *[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_State_Mobile Wikipedia]

{{GameSeries_Navbox}} {{Infobox company |image=Level Infinite lightmode.png |imagedark=Level_Infinite_darkmode.png |name=Level Infinite |industry=Professional Gaming League |foundeddate=2021-12-07 |headquarters={{Flag|nl}} Amsterdam
{{Flag|sg}} Singapore |employees=1,001 - 5,000 |products= |site=https://www.levelinfinite.com |discord= |twitter=LevelInfinite |facebook=LVLInfinite |youtube=@lvlinfinite |instagram=lvlinfinite }} ==Overview== '''Level Infinite''' is a video game publisher headquartered in {{Flag|nl}} Amsterdam and {{Flag|sg}} Singapore, and a subsidiary of [[Tencent Games]]. It was founded in December 2021 with the goal of delivering high-quality games to a global audience. '''Level Infinite''' has a team of experienced professionals with a wealth of experience in the games industry. They are committed to working with talented developers to create games that are both fun and engaging. In December 2021, [[Tencent Games]] launched '''Level Infinite''' as a new game publishing brand. Level Infinite took over the publishing of [[PUBG Mobile]] from [[Tencent Games]], and they have been responsible for the game's global growth and success. Under Level Infinite's leadership, [[PUBG Mobile]] has continued to grow in popularity. The game has been downloaded over 1 billion times worldwide, and it has over 50 million daily active users. [[PUBG Mobile]] is available in over 200 countries and regions, and it is localized into over 100 languages. '''Level Infinite''' has been committed to providing [[PUBG Mobile]] players with a great gaming experience. They have introduced a number of new features and content to the game, including new [[Portal:Maps|maps]], modes, and [[Portal:Weapons|weapons]]. They have also worked to improve the game's performance and stability. ==Events== {{TournamentsList|organizer={{PAGENAME}}|showTier=true}} ==References== {{Reflist}}

===Format===
* '''iQOO All Stars Cup - Season 4''' : ''January 22nd - 25th
** 24 teams.
** Squad , {{Abbr/TPP}}.
* ''' Group Stage''' : ''January 22nd - 23rd
** 24 teams.
** 12 invited teams , 12 teams from '''iQOO Community Cup.
** 2 matchdays.
** {{bgcolortext|up|Top 16 teams}} qualify for ''Grand Finals.
* '''Grand Finals''' : ''January 24th - 25th
** 16 teams.
** 2 matchdays.
** {{bgcolortext|up|Top 4 teams}} qualify for [[IQOO Pro Series/2024|iQOO Pro Series]]

===Format===
* '''iQOO Pro Series 2024''' : ''January 29th - Febraury 1st
** 24 teams.
** Squad , {{Abbr/TPP}}.
* '''Group Stage''' : ''January 29th - 30th
** 24 teams.
*** 4 teams from [[IQOO All Stars Cup/Season 4|All Stars Cup]], 20 invited teams.
** Teams are divided equally into 3 groups of 8 teams each.
*** Round-robin format.
** 2 matchdays.
** {{bgcolortext|up|Top 16 teams}} qualify for the '''[[#Grand Finals|Grand Finals]]'''.
* '''Grand Finals''' : ''January 31st - Febraury 1st
** 16 teams.
** 2 matchdays.
{{PUBGM 2023 Points System|showtb=true}}

* {{Bgcolortext|byeup|'''Top 14 teams'''}} qualify for {{LeagueIconSmall/iqoo india league|}} '''[[IQOO India League/2024|iQOO India League]]'''.

===Format===
* '''Lidoma Masters Showdown''' - ''March 11th - 19th
** 27 teams
** Squad, {{Abbr/TPP}}
* '''Semi Finals''' - ''March 11th - 14th 
** 27 teams
*** 23 invited teams
*** 4 qualified teams
** 4 matchdays
** 6 matches per day
** Teams are divided equally into 3 groups of 9 teams.
** Round-robin format.
** {{bgcolortext|up|Top 16 teams}} qualify for ''Grand Finals.
* '''Grand Finals''' - ''March 16th - 19th
** 16 teams
** 4 matchdays
** 6 matches per day
